title: rubus carpinifolius var. platyacanthus
intro: Rubus carpinifolius var. platyacanthus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a variety of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rubus carpinifolius var. platyacanthus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, as well as being used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and sore throats. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health ailments, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine to make jams, jellies, and other edible products.
